variant.phenotype_context: Lifetime cannabis use (LCU) instrument; within-family GWAS

locus_genes.variant_annotation: Intronic – sits between SLC2A13 (upstream, – strand) and LRRK2 (downstream, + strand) in a predicted regulatory region

LINC02555_lncRNA.note: Strongest and most consistent brain-wide effect

SLC2A13_protein_coding.note: Consistently UP in prefrontal and striatal regions; also strongest splice junction signal

LRRK2_protein_coding.direction_T_allele: mixed – UP in non-brain, small DOWN in reward/limbic regions
LRRK2_protein_coding.note: Directionally inconsistent across brain sub-regions; strongest net effect is slight DOWN in limbic/striatal areas

LRRK2_DT_lncRNA.note: Broad pan-tissue downregulation; lncRNA antisense to LRRK2; consistent

KIF21A_protein_coding.note: Strongest UP signal in cerebellum/Purkinje cells (q=+0.995)

dnase_brain.summary: T allele decreases DNase accessibility in glutamatergic neurons, neuronal stem cells, whole brain, dorsolateral PFC, posterior cingulate gyrus, and head of caudate nucleus. Increases accessibility in cerebellum and cerebellar cortex.

H3K4me1.direction: Predominantly INCREASES (T allele) in: dlPFC, cingulate gyrus, hippocampus layer, substantia nigra, temporal lobe, caudate nucleus
H3K4me1.notable: substantia nigra q=+0.900, hippocampus q=+0.900, caudate q=+0.883
H3K4me1.interpretation: T allele activates enhancers in dopaminergic/reward circuits

H3K27ac.direction: INCREASES in dlPFC, caudate nucleus, cingulate gyrus, substantia nigra, temporal lobe, hippocampus
H3K27ac.notable: dlPFC q=+0.879, cingulate gyrus q=+0.871, caudate q=+0.854, substantia nigra q=+0.854
H3K27ac.interpretation: Active enhancer activation in striatum and prefrontal cortex

enhancer_marks_brain.conclusion: Variant overlaps predicted enhancer active in striatum/dopamine pathway brain regions

splice_junctions.top_hit: SLC2A13 – all tissues highly positive (q ~0.98-0.99); includes brain (frontal cortex, motor neuron, glutamatergic neuron, cerebellum)
splice_junctions.interpretation: T allele increases SLC2A13 splice junction usage across all tissues

mendelian_randomization_interpretation.risk_allele: T (minor allele, MAF=0.145; confirmed from LCU GWAS context)

proposed_mechanism.step1: rs11174281-T allele activates enhancer elements in striatum, hippocampus, cingulate gyrus, and prefrontal cortex (H3K4me1 and H3K27ac increase in these regions)
proposed_mechanism.step2: This enhancer activation drives UP-regulation of SLC2A13 in prefrontal/striatal regions (a glucose transporter that modulates neuronal energy metabolism and may alter reward circuitry tone)
proposed_mechanism.step3: Simultaneously strongly DOWN-regulates LINC02555, a lncRNA with broad brain expression whose loss may de-repress neuronal gene programs related to motivation/reward
proposed_mechanism.step4: LRRK2-DT (antisense lncRNA to LRRK2) is consistently downregulated, potentially affecting LRRK2 expression regulation in dopamine neurons (LRRK2 itself shows subtle direction-specific effects by brain subregion)
proposed_mechanism.step5: Reduced chromatin accessibility in glutamatergic neurons and dorsolateral PFC suggests the variant remodels the local regulatory landscape in a cell-type-specific manner

mendelian_randomization_interpretation.best_mechanistic_hypothesis: rs11174281-T is a regulatory SNP that activates an intronic enhancer in the SLC2A13–LRRK2 locus on chr12. In striatal and prefrontal circuits (caudate, putamen, nucleus accumbens, dlPFC, substantia nigra), this enhancer drives up SLC2A13 expression and simultaneously silences LINC02555. SLC2A13 encodes a glucose/myoinositol transporter expressed in brain; altered transport capacity could modulate glutamatergic/dopaminergic neurotransmission and reward sensitivity – the neurobiological substrate most plausibly connecting this locus to lifetime cannabis use.
caveats: AlphaGenome scores are in silico – validation in human brain tissue needed
C>T and C>A are both modelled; the true risk allele from the LCU GWAS should be confirmed
LINC02555 function in brain is not well-characterised
LRRK2 effects are directionally inconsistent across replicates, limiting inference
The variant is intronic (in SLC2A13) so its target gene requires experimental validation
